rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 8 'ambition and length grow with practice' is the idiomatic skill-building sense, not the banned ceremony-noun; AE101 carve-out also permits practice-noun landing at M4 (this lecture's module). 'practitioner' (lines 16, 44) is the cultivated character word, allowed.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: AE101 quintet honored; M4 mood is 'curious readiness' (not sustained-unease), so the Sutherland/Risto-forward experimental-stance voice ('every send-off is an experiment'; 'That result is the point') is in-contract, with Boris-direct carrying the cancel rule ('stop it. You've got the data you need.').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Rough-cut drafts for articles.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Articles-only rule; target is a curriculum lecture, not an article draft.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit original when paraphrasing named technique.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The one named technique in body (Ronacher's three-pattern, line 24) is credited inline. Huryn and Klaassen are deliberately not named here (maintainer block routes their attribution to the M4 exercise and M1 Debrief); gap analysis is generic with no single attribution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses second-person throughout; no 'the student', 'the trainer', or delivery-mechanic lines above the cut.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Ronacher's three-pattern is named as a deliberate forward-reference (maintainer-confirmed); the experiment-metaphor terms (apparatus/setup/assay/data) are earned in-place on line 14 with their plain referents. No unearned maintainer/strategy term leaks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Over-hedge detector.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Cancel is legitimate' (line 34) reads as a practitioner rule, not reassurance: the elaboration ('stop it. You've got the data you need.') is operator guidance, and the maintainer watch-for explicitly frames it as 'what real operators do', not a fallback for nervous students. No 'no penalty / it's OK if' reassurance shapes present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Reference-artefact authoring rule; target is a lecture, not a reference artefact capturing a user-supplied verbal frame.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Only Ronacher is named once in body (line 24); under the one-mention cap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'Cancel is legitimate. Traces are data.' (line 34) is a slogan followed by a concrete boundary paragraph (lines 36-38) naming what to do and ending on a directional cue ('Any trace counts.'); the punch is kept and the carve-out sits downstream.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project strategy docs, NOT student-facing curriculum body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No defensive/positioning phrases ('not a vendor', 'not marketing', 'the real practitioners') and no 'vendor' token in body. 'practitioner vs tourist' (line 16) is a teaching contrast about stance, not a marketing-vs-vendor positioning leak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Watch out for planting jargon too early.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Terms named ahead of M5 (plan.md, verifier, reference artifact, Ronacher's three-pattern) appear in explanatory lecture body as deliberate forward-references, not in operational copy the student copies; they are plain mechanical names, not unearned LLM-internal jargon. Maintainer block confirms M5 owns the teaching.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Audit closer when trimming body-prose forcing function.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No dangling pronouns/demonstratives and no bypass-the-agent punchlines; closers ('Walk your system. Fill the gaps. Send it off. Read what comes back.') resolve cleanly against named referents.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Past tense for exercise recap sections — heading and body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'What happens after' (line 40) looks forward to M5, not back at a closed activity, so present/future tense is correct; this is not a 'What happened:' recap section. The arc recap (line 6) uses module-as-subject past tense ('M1 closed a bug...') appropriately.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Human-to-agent verbs stay peer-flat ('launch', 'send it off', 'steer', 'audits your setup'); no bark/order/command/whip/force-about-the-agent shapes.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Names what the student is becoming ('a practitioner... runs an agent as a test and reads the data', line 16); no corrective-gap or ability-tier pegging.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Experiment-metaphor terms are earned in-place (line 14). M-abbreviations are fine: this is an M4 lecture, past the M2 establishment point, so bare M1-M5 need no per-file earning.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Business-audience word ban (Agents 101 default).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Agents-101-default business-audience ban; AE101 audience is software-engineer ICs, and the file's earned-jargon terms are engineer-appropriate.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Memory vs context — use deliberately.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'your rules and context are the setup' (line 14) uses 'context' as ambient runtime state correctly; no 'context compounds' conflation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Golden path only in body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The cancel rule (lines 34-38) is forward operator guidance for the experiment, not a 'what if X fails' troubleshooting FAQ branch; body stays calm forward-moving narrative.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt-shape rule; this lecture contains no fenced prompt with output destination.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module. Compound-shape varies per module — never repeat same Debrief.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief/compound-move rule; this is a lecture with no Debrief section.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle another module's voice.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Voice is M4 curious-readiness / experimental-stance; no M1 joyful-creation or M5 unleashed-leverage smuggling.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't ask student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'read the result' framing is emotional/diagnostic payoff (read what came back, diagnose), not a manual error-catching diff ask; gap analysis routes the audit through Claude as a subagent (line 30).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Simulation personas never alone.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Self-study simulation rule; no simulation persona in this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No "What research says" callout boxes
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No research-callout boxes; the one named source (Ronacher) is inline.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Student moves stay in conversation with agent.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Student moves (launch, send off, audit-via-subagent, read the result) stay in conversation with the agent; no editor/terminal forcing function in body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No CLAUDE.md reference in body, so no layer to qualify.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No em-dashes in the body region (lines 1-47); the em-dashes present are all below the <!-- maintainer --> cut at line 48, which is exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Lecture exemption applies: a lecture's reader job is understanding, so declarative noun-phrase headers ('The spirit', 'The two-run arc', 'What happens after') are permitted.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use target's title, never filename.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No inline links to other curriculum surfaces in the body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No first student-authored write to a contested word here. 'subagent' (line 30) is used in its acting sense (Claude audits as a subagent); the memory/skill/rule collisions are not authored in this lecture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body is agent-shaped (agent x7 for autonomous work, Claude x2 for invocation/addressee, LLM x0); ratio matches an agent-shaped lecture about autonomous long-running work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No banned time-of-day anchors (tomorrow/overnight/this morning/tonight/yesterday). 'Today' is session-relative ('this run/now'), not a time-of-day anchor, and 'whenever the next M5 slot lands' is module-relative phrasing the rule recommends.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Reflection beats stay invisible in flow.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No self-aware pedagogy headers or contemplative-pause prefixes ('Meta-moment', 'Step back', 'sit with', 'let it land'); transitions carry their own action.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No meta-ask when student has already decided.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No 'ask me which one / name it in one sentence' meta-loop; gap analysis frames a question the student answers in flow, not a Claude-asks-student-what-they-decided loop.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: `.md` is default file type for student artifacts across curriculum.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No file-saving prompt in this lecture (plan.md is named as a packaging artifact, not a save instruction); no .txt/.docx substitution.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cut NVA: true but not moving student forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ~620-word body stays tight; each section moves the student toward the stance, the two-run arc, the one move, and the one rule. No administrative asides or maintainer reasoning leaking into body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body "read it" / "look at it" / "notice X" after chat-print = dead weight.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: 'read the result' / 'read what comes back' carry follow-on direction (diagnose, decide what to change), not bare 'read it' narration of a chat-print the student is already reading.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forward-callout closer: student as grammatical subject.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Forward callouts keep student as subject ('You'll use this move on every future agent hand-off, forever', line 30; 'You read what came back', line 42); no training-as-subject, coach-imperative, generic-they, or audience-shift role-gating.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Operational reminders live at moment of use, not only where introduced.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Per-module operational-setting reminder rule; the lecture carries no thinking-effort/model/mode/worktree setting that needs a moment-of-use restatement (the visible-timing line lives in the maintainer block, appropriate for a lecture).
